response.justification: Bogdan Szabo brings over 15 years of solid hands-on software engineering experience with genuine open-source contributions and cross-domain technical breadth. However, the role has a hard requirement of C1 German proficiency for client-facing consulting in a German-speaking market, and the candidate self-reports only 'Basic' German — a disqualifying gap on its own. Beyond the language barrier, the position is an executive-level Head of Development role requiring formal team leadership, IT architecture governance, UML modeling, requirements management, and compliance/security advisory responsibilities, none of which are substantively evidenced in a resume that reads almost entirely as an individual-contributor engineering track.
strengths: 15+ years of polyglot, full-stack engineering experience across embedded, mobile, web, and cloud domains demonstrates strong technical adaptability
Genuine open-source contributions (GISCollective, Fluent-Asserts, Trial) and a FOSS4G conference talk signal real alignment with open-source culture
Community knowledge-sharing activities (Selenium course, Refactoring Dojos, Web Internals workshop) show some capacity for mentorship and communication
concerns: German proficiency is self-reported as 'Basic', well below the mandatory C1 minimum required for client advisory and consulting engagement in this role
No demonstrated experience in formal team or project leadership, executive stakeholder management, or UML/architecture governance — core duties of this position
No visible background in IT security, risk management, or compliance considerations, which are explicitly listed as required responsibilities

key_factors.factor: German language deficiency (Basic vs. required C1)
Individual-contributor career track with no leadership or management evidence
Strong open-source background and broad technical depth
